PROPOSED SOLAR FARM — PLANNING STAGE DOCUMENTATION

Project: Sunnyside Solar Initiative
Status: Pre-Construction Planning

PLANNED RENEWABLE ENERGY:
The developer plans to install 2 MW of rooftop solar panels by 2028. It is intended that
the facility will achieve 40% of its electricity from renewable sources. Wind energy
integration is being considered for a future phase. The project will reduce CO2 emissions
once operational.

FUTURE CERTIFICATION:
The project intends to apply for LEED Silver certification upon completion. EDGE
certification will be pursued after construction is finished.

ENERGY EFFICIENCY:
An energy management system is planned for installation. The building design is intended
to achieve a 25% reduction in energy consumption compared to baseline. These systems
will be commissioned in 2028.

CURRENT STATUS:
At the time of this assessment, no renewable energy systems are installed. The site is
currently an empty lot in the preconstruction phase. No construction has started.
All environmental benefits described above are contingent on project completion.

NOTE FOR REVIEWERS:
This document describes planned activities only. No operational environmental performance
data is available.
